content:
# Recommendations — for iter-025 plan

## TOP — FBC glue: STRUCTURAL escalation, do NOT retry the functor-level `≪≫`-chain
The iter-024 prover ran the directive's decisive STUCK test and it FIRED AT BOTH ENDS of the
`ring_square_glue_natTrans` route:
- STATEMENT over-budget (`isDefEq`/`whnf` @200000hb @L1942, extendScalars/ModuleCat-diamond whnf at the
  `isoWhiskerRight (chartBaseChangeModuleReassocNat) (tilde.functor …)` seam; elaborates only at 800000hb).
- FOLD over-budget (`whnf` @200000hb; the three non-geom `pullbackSpecTildeNatIso` legs each cross
  `pullback (Spec _).obj (tilde M)` sheafification in the `.app M` distribution).

**Do NOT assign another prover round on the functor-level `≪≫`-chain natTrans + fold** — it has been
measured over-budget twice over; another helper/telescope round will reproduce the same two bombs.
Required structural moves (pick one, scope as a refactor or a carefully-shaped prover lane):
1. Give the three non-geom `pullbackSpecTildeNatIso` legs the SAME syntactic-`.app M`-by-construction
   treatment the geom leg got this iter (re-base each leg's relevant object def on its `…Nat.app (tilde M)`
   form), so the fold is defeq on all four legs. This is the direct extension of the successful
   `fbc-geom-reorder`.
2. OR assemble the glue iso as a single closed term (component-free) so no `.obj (tilde M)` defeq is ever
   forced, and find a statement phrasing whose `tilde.functor` whisker seam does not whnf the
   extendScalars/ModuleCat diamond.
Consider a `mathlib-analogist` (cross-domain) consult on "fold a composite-functor natural iso to a
component without whnf'ing a sheafification carrier" — this is now a 5+-iter structural wall, the
approach-shape signal the analogist exists for. The 5/6 closed scaffold + geom re-base +
`pullbackSpecTildeNatIso`/`_app` + `chartBaseChangeModuleReassoc_eq_natApp` are READY for whichever route.

## SNAP — Option-A `⊗_loc` re-base, from the now-green smaller baseline
The iter-024 obligation (recorded in the plan sidecar): iter-025 MUST dispatch a SNAP prover/refactor —
a 4th consecutive skip = avoidance. The build is restored (cold-green, 2442 jobs) and the file is smaller
post-split. The associator wall `tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_native` (@L1082, private, gates the public
`tensorObjAssoc_eq_localizedAssociator`) is the long-stuck (≈14-iter) dual-MonoidalCategory-instance
μ-divergence; Option-A (re-base `tensorObj`/unitor/braiding/associator onto `⊗_loc`, deleting the wall)
is the designed fix (`analogies/snap-instance-design.md`, ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB). NOTE: the Option-A refactor
has timed out 3× as a monolithic dispatch — decompose into per-def cold-build-gated steps (the file split
already de-risked this) or surface to the user if it 0-edits a 4th time.

## MEDIUM — cleanup (none block downstream; bundle into the FBC dead-apparatus excision iter)
- **Two misplaced `set_option maxHeartbeats` clusters** (FlatBaseChange.lean L2272 and L2307–2310): both
  scope to section doc-comments (`/-! ### Seam … -/`), NOT to `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` @L2322.
  Once that sorry is filled the theorem will run UNGUARDED at the default budget. These live in the
  COMPILE-DEAD `base_change_mate_*` apparatus already slated for a dedicated excision iter (archon-memory) —
  fix or excise together. `set_option … in` must directly precede the decl, not a section comment.
- **Duplicate stale module-doc headers**: both `SectionGradedRing.lean` and `SectionGradedRingLocalized.lean`
  carry the identical "Layer 1: tensor powers" title post-split; the Localized file should describe its
  monoidal-localization content.
- **Large proof-archaeology / commented-out blocks**: FBC L2000–2162 (~163L history in the glue sorry body),
  L1911–1970 (~60L commented natTrans), SNAP ~996–1082 and ~1382–1440; plus stale `iter-NNN`/`ROUTE (iter-018)`
  refs embedded in production docstrings. Trim when an editor/refactor lane next touches these files.

## Coverage debt (planner: restore blueprint correspondence; doctrine "when there is Lean there must be tex")
82 `lean_aux` nodes have no blueprint entry (`archon dag-query unmatched`). **No NEW debt this iter.** Bulk:
- **67 in `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.*`** (SNAP infra: `MonoidalPresheaf`, `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.{aL,aR,actLmap,actLmap_tmul,actM,…}`, `objRestrict*`, `uTensorEquiv`/`uTripleEquiv`/`uMod*` helpers, etc.) — long-standing SNAP monoidal-infra helpers in `SectionGradedRing*.lean`.
- **~15 FBC helpers**: `chartBaseChangeModuleReassocNat`, `chartBaseChangePushforwardNat`,
  `chartBaseChangeRestrictReassocNat`, `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_{hom,inv}_conjugateEquiv`,
  `*_concreteApply` rfl-helpers, the `base_change_mate_*` dead apparatus.
These need blueprint stubs (planner writes prose; review agent does not). Lowest-effort win: blueprint the
FBC `*Nat` helpers (they feed the live glue route). The SNAP `Modules.*` bulk can wait for the Option-A
re-base (which may delete several).

## Do-NOT-retry (blocked, structural change required first)
- `ring_square_glue_natTrans` functor-level `≪≫`-chain statement — over-budget; needs a different phrasing.
- The `congrArg (Iso.app · M)` fold of the glue — over-budget on the three non-geom legs; needs all-legs-syntactic
  or component-free assembly.
- SNAP `tensorObjAssoc_hK_lhs_native` bridge proof — irreducible dual-instance μ-divergence; only Option-A
  (delete the wall) advances it, NOT another bridge-proof attempt.
